{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Pastry Cashier",
  "description": "Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pastry Cashier

Job Location: Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a friendly and detail-oriented Pastry Cashier to provide excellent customer service, process sales transactions, and support the daily operations of the pastry shop.
	The ideal candidate is courteous, reliable, and passionate about creating a positive customer experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome customers and assist with product selection.
	Process cash, card, and electronic payments accurately.
	Handle customer inquiries and resolve complaints professionally.
	Maintain accurate records of sales transactions.
	Ensure pastry displays are clean, organized, and well-stocked.
	Support daily opening and closing procedures.


Requirements


	OND, HND, SSCE, or equivalent qualification.
	Strong communication and customer service skills.
	Basic numerical and cash-handling abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Previous cashier or retail experience is an advantage but not required.


Key Competencies:


	Customer Service
	Cash Handling
	Communication Skills
	Attention to Detail
	Time Management
	Teamwork
	Professionalism.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: recruitment@domeoresources.org using the Job Position and State as the subject of the email.",

Pastry Cashier Job at Domeo Resources International (DRI)




        
    

        
        Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pastry Cashier

Job Location: Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a friendly and detail-oriented Pastry Cashier to provide excellent customer service, process sales transactions, and support the daily operations of the pastry shop.
	The ideal candidate is courteous, reliable, and passionate about creating a positive customer experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome customers and assist with product selection.
	Process cash, card, and electronic payments accurately.
	Handle customer inquiries and resolve complaints professionally.
	Maintain accurate records of sales transactions.
	Ensure pastry displays are clean, organized, and well-stocked.
	Support daily opening and closing procedures.


Requirements


	OND, HND, SSCE, or equivalent qualification.
	Strong communication and customer service skills.
	Basic numerical and cash-handling abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Previous cashier or retail experience is an advantage but not required.


Key Competencies:


	Customer Service
	Cash Handling
	Communication Skills
	Attention to Detail
	Time Management
	Teamwork
	Professionalism.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position and State as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Marketing Manager",
  "description": "Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Manager

Job Location: Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative and results-driven Marketing Manager to develop and execute marketing strategies that increase brand awareness, attract customers, and drive business growth.
	The ideal candidate is innovative, analytical, and capable of leading marketing initiatives across multiple channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement marketing campaigns and promotional activities.
	Manage the company&#39;s brand presence across digital and traditional channels.
	Create and oversee content for social media, advertising, and marketing materials.
	Monitor market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities.
	Track campaign performance and prepare marketing reports.
	Collaborate with sales and operations teams to support business objectives.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree, HND, or OND in Marketing, Business Administration, Communications, or a related field.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing and social media platforms.
	Ability to analyze data and make informed decisions.
	Previous marketing experience is an advantage but not required.


Key Competencies:


	Marketing Strategy
	Digital Marketing
	Communication Skills
	Creativity
	Data Analysis
	Leadership
	Project Management.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@domeoresources.org using the Job Position and state as the subject of the mail.",

Marketing Manager Job at Domeo Resources International




        
    

        
        Domeo Resources International (DRI) is a prolific organisation which proffers HR and Management Consultancy solutions premised on excellence and innovation. DRI analyses various organisational problems, develops improvement plans, deploys those plans and monitors the plans to ensure improved organisational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Manager

Job Location: Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a creative and results-driven Marketing Manager to develop and execute marketing strategies that increase brand awareness, attract customers, and drive business growth.
	The ideal candidate is innovative, analytical, and capable of leading marketing initiatives across multiple channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement marketing campaigns and promotional activities.
	Manage the company's brand presence across digital and traditional channels.
	Create and oversee content for social media, advertising, and marketing materials.
	Monitor market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities.
	Track campaign performance and prepare marketing reports.
	Collaborate with sales and operations teams to support business objectives.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree, HND, or OND in Marketing, Business Administration, Communications, or a related field.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing and social media platforms.
	Ability to analyze data and make informed decisions.
	Previous marketing experience is an advantage but not required.


Key Competencies:


	Marketing Strategy
	Digital Marketing
	Communication Skills
	Creativity
	Data Analysis
	Leadership
	Project Management.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and state as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior HR Officer",
  "description": "BURN is the largest vertically integrated modern cookstove company in the world, providing world-class R&amp;D, manufacturing, and carbon offset projects from our HQ in Kenya and across 10 countries in Africa. We are working to create a world where cooking positively impacts all life on earth by producing the world&rsquo;s most efficient biomass, liquid fuel, and electric cookstoves. Senior HR Officer Job at BURN Nigeria




        
    

        
        BURN is the largest vertically integrated modern cookstove company in the world, providing world-class R&D, manufacturing, and carbon offset projects from our HQ in Kenya and across 10 countries in Africa. We are working to create a world where cooking positively impacts all life on earth by producing the world’s most efficient biomass, liquid fuel, and electric cookstoves. Our team of 900 people - 51% of whom are women - is on track to produce and sell over 150,000 stoves per month in 2022 at our solar-powered facility in Kenya.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior HR Officer

Job Location: Kano, Nigeria
Job type: Full Time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	BURN operates a factory in Kano, making world-class clean cooking stoves. We're looking for a Senior HR Officer who will play a crucial supporting role within the Human Resources functions in Kano, Nigeria.
	This individual will be responsible for assisting in various administrative and operational tasks to ensure the smooth functioning of HR processes and the overall well-being of the workforce, primarily for Kano, but more broadly in the Nigerian and group operation. 


Duties and Responsibilities


	Onboarding process: the employee will oversee the onboarding process for all new employees of BURN, including inducting them through all documentation (contracts, policies, rules, and procedures). 
	Dealing with any HR-related issues brought up by employees and escalating them to management where necessary. 
	Recruiting both casual and full-time employees, up to and including managerial staff. 
	Reviewing company policies and training the employees on the changes. 
	Collecting all documentation required for contract preparation from all new employees. 
	Monitoring contract expiry dates by managing the electronic employee data sheet and ensuring that employees do not work without valid contracts. 
	Managing the staff medical allowance scheme.  Ensuring that the claims presented are authentic and that reimbursement is made. 
	Performing the correct exit process for all employees and contractors, ensuring that all documentation is signed off before exit, medical for factory employees exiting the company is done, and all the items that belong to the company are returned in good condition. 
	Coordinating the medical check-up for all employees. 
	Keeping the training calendar updated and ensuring that training needs are well captured. 
	Performing any other duties as may be assigned by your manager from time to time.


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree in Industrial Relations or any other relevant course with at least 5 years of HR experience in the manufacturing sector. 
	Sound knowledge and understanding of the Nigerian Labour legislation. 
	Proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and PowerPoint. 
	Strong people management and communication skills. 
	Excellent organisational skills. 
	Ability to commute or relocate to Kano. 
	Membership in any recognised HR professional body is an added advantage. 
	Fluency in English required, together with proficiency in Hausa.


Note


	Qualified Female Candidates encouraged to Apply
	BURN does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process (application, interview, meeting, processing, training, or any other fees).
